ntas, Edward A. Lee, Carol Gobel. "Heterogeneous Composition of Models of Computation". Future Generation Computer Systems , 25(5):552-560, May 2009. Abstract A model of computation (MoC) is a formal abstraction of execution in a computer. There is a

abstraction of execution in a computer. There is a need for composing diverse MoCs in e-science. Kepler, which is based on Ptolemy II, is a scientific workflow environment that allows for MoC composition. This paper explains how MoCs are combined in

ion. This paper explains how MoCs are combined in Kepler and Ptolemy II and analyzes which combinations of MoCs are currently possible and useful. It demonstrates the approach by combining MoCs involving dataflow and finite state machines. The result

ing dataflow and finite state machines. The resulting classification should be relevant to other workflow environments wishing to combine multiple MoCs (available at http://ptolemy.org/heterogeneousMoCs ). Keywords: Model of computation; Scientific w
